name: Allariz - Verin Return
region_intro: Road cycling around Allariz offers diverse landscapes, from river plains and mixed woodlands to areas with significant elevation changes. The region is characterized by the winding Arnoia River Valley, rolling hills, and granite mountains to the north. Allariz is part of a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, featuring "Veigas" (river plains), "Touzas" (meadows and cultivated areas), and "Sebes" (mixed forests). This variety provides both challenging climbs and scenic, flatter sections for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Allariz The most popular road cycling route is Allariz - Verin Return, a 78.1 miles (125.6 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 58 minutes to complete. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Junta da Tona Cow Sculpture – Alto do Furriolo climb loop from Val da Rabeda, a difficult 45.3 miles (72.9 km) path. It offers views of the rolling hills and the granite mountains to the north. Local road cyclists also love the Bico do Ceo Pastry Shop – Acea River Beach loop from Allariz, a 44.4 miles (71.4 km) trail leading through the Arnoia River Valley and mixed woodlands, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Allariz is defined by river plains, mixed woodlands, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Allariz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 21 reviews. More than 676 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Allariz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Allariz?
A: Road cycling around Allariz offers a diverse landscape, ranging from the river plains of the Arnoia River Valley to mixed woodlands and areas with significant elevation changes. You'll encounter rolling hills and, to the north, the granite mountains of Allariz, providing both challenging climbs and scenic, flatter sections.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Allariz?
A: While many routes around Allariz are rated moderate to difficult, the region's diverse terrain does offer sections that can be more accessible. For a less strenuous experience, consider exploring the flatter river plains within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1605417/road-cycling-routes-around-allariz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, particularly the 'Veigas' along the Limia and Antela Rivers, which are characterized by herbaceous vegetation and shrubs.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in Allariz?
A: The road cycling routes in Allariz primarily range from moderate to difficult. Out of 41 available routes, 30 are classified as moderate and 11 as difficult. These challenging routes often feature significant elevation gains and can include some unpaved segments requiring good fitness.

Q: What historical and natural landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant points of interest. You might encounter the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3004027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Freixo Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5420489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Santa María la Real de Xunqueira de Ambía&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3132926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Allariz?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Allariz are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 45.3-mile (72.9 km) route, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Junta da Tona Cow Sculpture – Alto do Furriolo climb loop from Val da Rabeda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Allariz?
A: Allariz offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be excellent, though it's advisable to start earlier in the day to avoid the midday heat, especially on longer or more exposed routes.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities near road cycling trailheads in Allariz?
A: Allariz, being a town with a focus on outdoor activities, generally offers parking options. You can typically find parking in and around the town center, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. Specific trailheads outside the town may have smaller designated parking areas, so it's always good to check local maps or route descriptions for details.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes in Allariz?
A: While road cycling routes are generally on paved surfaces, the region's natural areas are often dog-friendly. If you plan to stop and explore natural parks or trails off the main road, ensure your dog is on a leash and that you adhere to local regulations regarding pets in protected areas, such as the Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Allariz?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Allariz, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 21 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the scenic Arnoia River Valley to the challenging climbs in the rolling hills and mountains, as well as the well-maintained paved surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Allariz itself has a charming historic quarter with various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Many routes pass through or near smaller villages where you might find local establishments. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1272144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bico do Ceo Pastry Shop – Acea River Beach loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt; starts near a well-known pastry shop, offering a convenient stop.

Q: How long do road cycling routes in Allariz typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in Allariz varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40366864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Junta da Tona Cow Sculpture – Hórreos of A Merca loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt; (26.1 miles), can take around 2 hours. More challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926967594/allariz-verin-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Allariz - Verin Return&lt;/a&gt; (78.1 miles), can take nearly 6 hours due to significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve, and how does it impact cycling?
A: The Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve is a UNESCO-designated area known for its cultural value and sustainable resource use. For cyclists, this means riding through distinct natural landscapes: 'Veigas' (river plains), 'Touzas' (meadows and cultivated areas), and 'Sebes' (mixed forests). These diverse habitats offer varied scenery and a rich backdrop for your rides, emphasizing the region's commitment to preserving its natural beauty.
